site.title: Computer Vision arXiv Digest
site.description: Daily ranked top 5 cs.CV papers with expandable full listings.

top5.title: HiDream-O1-Image: A Natively Unified Image Generative Foundation Model with Pixel-level Unified Transformer
top5.summary: A major image generation foundation model that pioneers end-to-end pixel-space Diffusion Transformers. Maps raw pixels, text tokens, and task conditions into a single shared token space, replacing the fragmented VAE+text-encoder pipelines that dominate current generative models with a natively unified architecture.

top5.title: The first global agricultural field boundary map at 10m resolution
top5.summary: Landmark dataset: 3.17 billion field boundary polygons across 241 countries at 10m resolution, produced via U-Net segmentation on Sentinel-2 mosaics. Validated against ground truth in 24 countries with mean recall of 0.85. First globally consistent field-level analysis unit for crop monitoring and food security.

top5.title: SenseNova-U1: Unifying Multimodal Understanding and Generation with NEO-unify Architecture
top5.summary: A native unified multimodal model that bridges the persistent understanding-generation divide in VLMs. Introduces NEO-unify architecture to replace cascaded pipelines and fragmented representations, targeting the structural limitation that prevents emergence of true multimodal intelligence.

top5.title: AlphaGRPO: Unlocking Self-Reflective Multimodal Generation in UMMs via Decompositional Verifiable Reward
top5.summary: Applies Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)—the RL algorithm behind DeepSeek-R1—to multimodal generation without cold-start. Enables reasoning-driven text-to-image generation that infers implicit user intent, and self-reflective refinement where the model autonomously diagnoses and corrects its own outputs.

top5.title: GLM-5V-Turbo: Toward a Native Foundation Model for Multimodal Agents
top5.summary: Next-generation multimodal agent foundation model from the GLM team that integrates visual perception as a core component of reasoning, planning, tool use, and execution. Handles heterogeneous contexts including images, videos, webpages, documents, and GUIs natively.

top5.title: Qwen-Image-2.0 Technical Report
top5.summary: Alibaba's comprehensive technical report on Qwen-Image-2.0, a major next-generation text-to-image model. Details architecture, training methodology, and extensive benchmarks showing competitive or state-of-the-art performance across generation quality, text rendering, and instruction following.

top5.title: Pixal3D: Pixel-Aligned 3D Generation from Images
top5.summary: SIGGRAPH 2026 paper introducing a pixel-aligned framework for generating high-quality 3D assets from single or few input images. Leverages pixel-level correspondence between 2D and 3D representations for sharper geometry and texture fidelity compared to prior feed-forward 3D generators.

top5.title: Personal Visual Context Learning in Large Multimodal Models
top5.summary: From Kristen Grauman's group at UT Austin, this work enables LMMs to learn personal visual contexts (objects, people, places) from user-provided images, allowing personalized reasoning without full fine-tuning. Opens the door for privacy-preserving on-device multimodal personalization.

top5.title: PhyGround: Benchmarking Physical Reasoning in Generative World Models
top5.summary: A massive 56-page systematic benchmark evaluating physical reasoning capabilities of generative video and world models. Covers gravity, collision, fluid dynamics, and object permanence across 40+ tasks, revealing significant gaps between current models and human-level physical intuition.

top5.title: Alice v1: Distillation-Enhanced Video Generation Surpassing Closed-Source Models
top5.summary: Claims to surpass closed-source video generation models through a novel distillation pipeline. Demonstrates that careful teacher-student training can produce open-weight video generators competitive with proprietary systems, with strong implications for democratizing video generation.

top5.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
top5.summary: SIGGRAPH 2026. Proposes a zero-shot framework for joint camera trajectory and 3D object motion control in video generation, eliminating the need for task-specific fine-tuning. From the groups of Philip Torr and Ivan Laptev.

top5.title: Relit-LiVE: Relight Video by Jointly Learning Environment Video
top5.summary: SIGGRAPH 2026. Novel approach to video relighting that jointly models temporally-varying environment lighting and scene appearance, enabling realistic re-lighting of dynamic videos.

top5.title: ViTok-v2: Scaling Native Resolution Auto-Encoders to 5 Billion Parameters
top5.summary: Scales visual tokenization auto-encoders to 5 billion parameters for native-resolution image/video encoding, pushing the frontier of autoregressive visual generation quality.

top5.title: MUSE: Resolving Manifold Misalignment in Visual Tokenization via Topological Orthogonality
top5.summary: ICML 2026 main track. Addresses manifold misalignment in visual tokenization by enforcing topological orthogonality constraints, improving reconstruction fidelity and downstream generation quality.

top5.title: Large Vision-Language Models Get Lost in Attention
top5.summary: ICML 2026. Reveals how LVLMs can 'get lost' in their attention mechanisms, causing failures in vision-language grounding. Provides analysis and diagnostic insights for improving multimodal model design.

top5.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
top5.summary: Zero-shot framework for jointly controlling camera trajectory and 3D object motion in video generation. Achieves disentangled control without per-scene optimization or additional training. Accepted at SIGGRAPH 2026.

top5.title: Earth-o1: A Grid-free Observation-native Atmospheric World Model
top5.summary: Builds a foundational atmospheric world model that operates directly on sparse native observations without gridded interpolation. A cross-domain CV application with major implications for weather and climate prediction, backed by a large international collaboration.

top5.title: Continuous-Time Distribution Matching for Few-Step Diffusion Distillation
top5.summary: Introduces a principled continuous-time formulation for diffusion distillation that matches full distributions rather than single trajectories. Enables high-quality few-step sampling with theoretical grounding. Includes Ming-Ming Cheng among authors.

top5.title: Revisiting Uncertainty: On Evidential Learning for Partially Relevant Video Retrieval
top5.summary: Applies evidential deep learning to video retrieval, explicitly modeling uncertainty when query-video relevance is partial. Provides both theoretical analysis and practical improvements for retrieval reliability. Accepted at ICML 2026.

top5.title: Render, Don't Decode: Weight-Space World Models with Latent Structural Disentanglement
top5.summary: Proposes a radically different world model that operates in weight space rather than pixel space, rendering states through weight dynamics instead of decoding latents. Introduces latent structural disentanglement for improved generalization. A thorough 35-page treatment of a bold new direction.

top5.title: Syn4D: A Multiview Synthetic 4D Dataset
top5.summary: Oxford VGG introduces a massive synthetic 4D dataset with 210 scenes and 30K images for generalizable 4D reconstruction. Addresses the critical data bottleneck in 4D vision with rigorous benchmarking across SOTA methods.

top5.title: OpenSearch-VL: An Open Recipe for Training Multimodal Search Agents with Reinforcement Learning
top5.summary: Tsinghua/BAAI presents an open recipe for multimodal search agents using RL. Open-source models, data, and training pipeline make competitive multimodal agent training accessible and reproducible.

top5.title: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers
top5.summary: Identifies and mitigates outlier tokens that bottleneck DiT inference, enabling faster image/video generation with no quality degradation. Directly applicable to deployed DiT-based generators.

top5.title: Action-Guided Neural Rendering of Subsurface Scattering Materials
top5.summary: Novel inverse rendering framework for subsurface scattering materials using action-guided optimization. Tackles a long-standing hard problem in neural rendering with demonstrated quality improvements.

top5.title: ReViT-TTA: Recurrent Test-Time Adaptation for Vision Transformers
top5.summary: Introduces recurrent test-time adaptation specifically designed for ViT architectures, addressing distribution shift in deployed vision models with a ViT-native approach outperforming prior TTA methods.

top5.title: UniCorrn: Unified Correspondence Transformer Across 2D and 3D
top5.summary: First unified correspondence model with shared weights across 2D-2D, 2D-3D, and 3D-3D matching. Introduces a novel dual-stream Transformer decoder that decouples appearance and positional features. Achieves +8% on 7Scenes (2D-3D) and +10% on 3DLoMatch (3D-3D) in registration recall, while maintaining competitive 2D-2D performance.

top5.title: Large Language Models are Universal Reasoners for Visual Generation
top5.summary: Proposes UniReasoner, a framework that leverages an LLM as a universal reasoner to close the understanding-generation gap in text-to-image models. Uses a Draft-Evaluate-Diffuse pipeline where the LLM generates a visual draft, critiques it for prompt consistency, and conditions a diffusion model on the joint signals. Improves GenEval from 0.79 to 0.88 without changing the diffusion backbone.

top5.title: Mantis: Mamba-native Tuning is Efficient for 3D Point Cloud Foundation Models
top5.summary: First Mamba-native parameter-efficient fine-tuning framework for 3D point cloud foundation models. Introduces State-Aware Adapter (SAA) that injects task-conditioned control into selective state-space updates, and Dual-Serialization Consistency Distillation (DSCD) for stable training. Achieves better-than-full-finetuning results with only ~5% trainable parameters, preserving Mamba's linear complexity.

top5.title: Audio-Visual Intelligence in Large Foundation Models
top5.summary: Comprehensive survey of audio-visual intelligence in large foundation models, covering architectures, training strategies, and downstream tasks across the audio-visual landscape. Provides a unified taxonomy and identifies key challenges and future directions at the intersection of vision and audio understanding.

top5.title: StateVLM: A State-Aware Vision-Language Model for Robotic Affordance Reasoning
top5.summary: Introduces a state-aware vision-language model that explicitly models object states and affordances for robotic manipulation. Enables reasoning about how object states change during interaction, improving performance on robotic affordance reasoning benchmarks by grounding visual understanding in physical state transitions.

top5.title: End-to-End Autoregressive Image Generation with 1D Semantic Tokenizer
top5.summary: ICML 2026 Spotlight. Proposes a 1D semantic tokenizer that enables end-to-end autoregressive image generation, achieving strong results with a streamlined architecture that bypasses the traditional 2D layout constraints.

top5.title: Rethinking Model Selection in VLM Through the Lens of Gromov-Wasserstein Distance
top5.summary: CVPR 2026 Highlight. Introduces a novel framework using Gromov-Wasserstein distance for principled model selection in vision-language models, addressing the challenge of matching model capabilities to downstream tasks.

top5.title: Visual Implicit Autoregressive Modeling
top5.summary: ICML 2026. Presents a visual implicit autoregressive model that learns to generate images by modeling the joint distribution of visual tokens in an implicit order, achieving competitive results against explicit raster-order approaches.

top5.title: SplAttN: Bridging 2D and 3D with Gaussian Soft Splatting and Attention for Point Cloud Completion
top5.summary: ICML 2026 Spotlight. Proposes a novel point cloud completion method that bridges 2D and 3D representations through Gaussian soft splatting and attention mechanisms, achieving state-of-the-art on shape completion benchmarks.

top5.title: Jailbreaking Vision-Language Models Through the Visual Modality
top5.summary: ICML 2026. Demonstrates that VLMs can be jailbroken through adversarial perturbations in the visual modality alone, revealing critical safety vulnerabilities in multimodal systems.

top5.title: Let ViT Speak: Generative Language-Image Pre-training
top5.summary: Proposes a novel paradigm that trains Vision Transformers to directly generate language tokens for vision-language tasks, flipping the standard LLaVA-style architecture. Eliminates the need for an LLM backbone by enabling ViTs to speak, achieving strong results in image captioning and VQA with a unified generative pre-training approach.

top5.title: End-to-End Autoregressive Image Generation with 1D Semantic Tokenizer
top5.summary: ICML 2026 Spotlight. Introduces a 1D semantic tokenizer that compresses images into compact 1D sequences, enabling efficient end-to-end autoregressive image generation without the need for 2D grid tokenization. Achieves state-of-the-art generation quality with significantly fewer tokens.

top5.title: UniVidX: A Unified Multimodal Framework for Versatile Video Generation via Diffusion Priors
top5.summary: SIGGRAPH 2026 (ACM ToG). A unified framework for video generation that leverages diffusion priors to enable versatile multimodal control including text, image, and video inputs. Supports diverse generation tasks within a single architecture.

top5.title: Colorful-Noise: Training-Free Low-Frequency Noise Manipulation for Color-Based Conditional Image Generation
top5.summary: SIGGRAPH 2026. A training-free method for color-conditioned image generation that manipulates low-frequency components of the noise latent in pre-trained diffusion models. Enables precise color control without any fine-tuning, representing an elegant and practical contribution to controllable generation.

top5.title: When Do Diffusion Models learn to Generate Multiple Objects?
top5.summary: ICML 2026. Investigates the training dynamics of diffusion models for multi-object generation, revealing at which stages of training models learn to compose multiple objects. Provides insights into compositional generation failures and suggests training strategies for improvement.

top5.summary: NVIDIA releases Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, an efficient open multimodal model extending their open AI ecosystem. The model delivers strong performance across vision, language, and multimodal understanding tasks while maintaining a small parameter footprint.

top5.title: VibeToken: Scaling 1D Image Tokenizers and Autoregressive Models for Dynamic Resolution Generations
top5.summary: CVPR'26 accepted (Sony Research). Introduces a resolution-agnostic 1D Transformer tokenizer that encodes images into 32-256 dynamic tokens. VibeToken-Gen synthesizes 1024x1024 images with only 64 tokens at 3.94 gFID, achieving 63.4x fewer FLOPs than LlamaGen while narrowing the gap to diffusion models.

top5.title: Power Foam: Unifying Real-Time Differentiable Ray Tracing and Rasterization
top5.summary: Google research presents a differentiable 3D representation unifying foam-based ray tracing with rasterization efficiency. Generalizes Voronoi foams to bounded power diagrams, adds oriented surface formulation, and achieves rasterization performance competitive with 3DGS while preserving state-of-the-art ray tracing.

top5.title: A Systematic Post-Train Framework for Video Generation
top5.summary: Proposes a comprehensive post-training framework for video diffusion models with four stages: SFT for instruction-following, GRPO-based RLHF for perceptual quality and temporal coherence, prompt enhancement via a specialized LM, and inference optimization. Provides a practical blueprint for scalable post-training pipelines.

top5.title: The Thinking Pixel: Recursive Sparse Reasoning in Multimodal Diffusion Latents
top5.summary: Introduces a recursive sparse mixture-of-experts framework into diffusion models, using a gating network to dynamically select specialized neural modules conditioned on visual tokens, diffusion timestep, and conditioning. Demonstrates superiority on ImageNet generation, GenEval, and DPG benchmarks.

top5.title: World-R1: Reinforcing 3D Constraints for Text-to-Video Generation
top5.summary: Microsoft framework aligning video generation with 3D constraints via reinforcement learning (Flow-GRPO). Uses pre-trained 3D foundation models and VLMs for structural feedback without altering architecture. Periodic decoupled training balances geometric consistency with scene fluidity. Code released.

top5.title: Tuna-2: Pixel Embeddings Beat Vision Encoders for Multimodal Understanding and Generation
top5.summary: Native unified multimodal model operating directly on pixel embeddings, eliminating pretrained vision encoders (VAE, representation encoders). Shows encoder-free design achieves stronger multimodal understanding at scale, competing with latent-space approaches for both generation and perception.

top5.title: Hallo-Live: Real-Time Streaming Joint Audio-Video Avatar Generation with Asynchronous Dual-Stream and Human-Centric Preference Distillation
top5.summary: Streaming framework for joint audio-visual avatar generation at 20.38 FPS with 0.94s latency on 2 H200 GPUs (16x throughput, 99x lower latency than teacher). Introduces Future-Expanding Attention for causal articulation and HP-DMD for preference-guided distillation.

top5.title: Zero-to-CAD: Agentic Synthesis of Interpretable CAD Programs at Million-Scale Without Real Data
top5.summary: Agentic framework synthesizing ~1M executable CAD construction sequences by embedding LLM in feedback-driven CAD environment. Releases 100K curated high-quality models. Fine-tuned VLM reconstructs editable CAD from multi-view images, outperforming GPT-5.2.

top5.title: MuSS: A Large-Scale Dataset and Cinematic Narrative Benchmark for Multi-Shot Subject-to-Video Generation
top5.summary: Large-scale dataset from 3,000+ movies for multi-shot video and subject-to-video generation. Progressive captioning pipeline eliminates contextual conflicts; cross-shot matching eradicates copy-paste shortcuts. Introduces Cinematic Narrative Benchmark with Anti-Copy-Paste Variance metric.

top5.title: GenMatter: Perceiving Physical Objects with Generative Matter Models
top5.summary: CVPR 2026. Tenenbaum/Freeman team proposes a generative model that hierarchically groups motion cues and appearance features into particles representing physical matter. Validated across random-dot kinematograms, camouflaged objects, and naturalistic video — unifying motion-based perception grounded in human vision principles.

top5.title: OccDirector: Language-Guided Behavior and Interaction Generation in 4D Occupancy Space
top5.summary: Generates 4D occupancy dynamics for autonomous driving simulation conditioned solely on natural language. Introduces OccInteract-85k dataset with multi-level language instructions and a VLM-based evaluation benchmark, shifting from appearance synthesis to language-driven behavior orchestration.

top5.title: Towards Safe Mobility: A Unified Transportation Foundation Model enabled by Open-Ended Vision-Language Dataset
top5.summary: Introduces LTD (11.6K VQA pairs from heterogeneous roadside cameras) and UniVLT, a transportation foundation model unifying microscopic autonomous driving reasoning with macroscopic traffic analysis via curriculum-based knowledge transfer.

top5.title: SS3D: End2End Self-Supervised 3D from Web Videos
top5.summary: Web-scale SfM-based self-supervision pipeline for feed-forward 3D estimation from monocular video. Pretrained on ~100M YouTube-8M frames with multi-view filtering and curriculum sampling, yielding strong cross-domain zero-shot transfer.

top5.title: SpaMEM: Benchmarking Dynamic Spatial Reasoning via Perception-Memory Integration in Embodied Environments
top5.summary: Large-scale diagnostic benchmark (10.6M images, 1,000 procedural houses, 15 tasks) isolating spatial belief evolution via action-conditioned scene transformations. Reveals VLM bottlenecks in visual memory maintenance vs. textual scaffolding dependency.

top5.title: Sapiens2
top5.summary: ICLR 2026. A 0.4-5B parameter transformer family for human-centric vision at up to 4K resolution. Combines masked image reconstruction with self-distilled contrastive pretraining on 1B curated images, setting new SOTA on pose (+4 mAP), body-part segmentation (+24.3 mIoU), normal estimation (-45.6% angular error), and adds pointmap/albedo tasks.

top5.title: Context Unrolling in Omni Models
top5.summary: A unified multimodal model natively trained on text, images, videos, 3D geometry, and hidden representations. Introduces Context Unrolling, where the model explicitly reasons across multiple modal representations before producing predictions, enabling in-context generation of text, image, video, and 3D geometry.

top5.title: Building a Precise Video Language with Human-AI Oversight
top5.summary: CVPR 2026 Highlight. Defines a structured video captioning specification with hundreds of visual primitives co-developed with professional filmmakers. Introduces CHAI (Critique-based Human-AI Oversight) framework where experts revise model pre-captures, yielding a caption model that outperforms Gemini-3.1-Pro and fine-tunes Wan VLMs for 400-word cinematography prompts.

top5.title: WorldMark: A Unified Benchmark Suite for Interactive Video World Models
top5.summary: First standardized benchmark for interactive image-to-video world models. Provides a unified WASD-style action-mapping layer across six models, 500 evaluation cases across difficulty tiers and viewpoints, and a modular toolkit for Visual Quality, Control Alignment, and World Consistency metrics. Launches World Model Arena for live leaderboards.

top5.title: Vista4D: Video Reshooting with 4D Point Clouds
top5.summary: CVPR 2026. A video reshooting framework that grounds input video and target cameras in 4D point clouds with static pixel segmentation and 4D reconstruction. Re-synthesizes scenes from new camera trajectories while preserving content appearance. Generalizes to dynamic scene expansion and 4D recomposition.

top5.title: Image Generators are Generalist Vision Learners
top5.summary: Shows that image-generation pretraining can serve as a unified interface for perception, with lightweight instruction tuning yielding state-of-the-art or competitive zero-shot results across 2D and 3D vision tasks while preserving generation ability.

top5.title: LLaDA2.0-Uni: Unifying Multimodal Understanding and Generation with Diffusion Large Language Model
top5.summary: Presents a natively unified diffusion LLM stack for multimodal understanding, image generation, and editing, combining discrete visual tokenization, a MoE diffusion backbone, and a decoder for interleaved reasoning and generation.

top5.title: SpaCeFormer: Fast Proposal-Free Open-Vocabulary 3D Instance Segmentation
top5.summary: Introduces a proposal-free open-vocabulary 3D instance segmentation model that runs in 0.14 seconds per scene and pairs it with a 3.0M-caption dataset, substantially improving zero-shot 3D segmentation performance.

top5.title: GeoRelight: Learning Joint Geometrical Relighting and Reconstruction with Flexible Multi-Modal Diffusion Transformers
top5.summary: Unifies human relighting and 3D geometry estimation in a single diffusion-transformer framework, using a distortion-free depth representation and mixed synthetic-real training to improve physical consistency over sequential pipelines.

top5.title: Exploring Spatial Intelligence from a Generative Perspective
top5.summary: Introduces GSI-Bench to measure generative spatial intelligence and shows that fine-tuning unified multimodal models on spatially grounded image-editing tasks improves both spatial generation and downstream spatial understanding.

top5.summary: Presents arbitrary-view 3D reconstruction driven by a video diffusion model, targeting stronger novel-view synthesis and geometry recovery from sparse visual input.

top5.title: CityRAG: Stepping Into a City via Spatially-Grounded Video Generation

top5.title: RF-HiT: Rectified Flow Hierarchical Transformer for General Medical Image Segmentation
top5.summary: Proposes a rectified-flow hierarchical Transformer for general medical image segmentation, aiming to improve robustness and cross-dataset performance across varied medical imaging tasks.

top5.title: OneVL: One-Step Latent Reasoning and Planning with Vision-Language Explanation
top5.summary: OneVL is notable for showing that latent chain-of-thought can beat explicit chain-of-thought in autonomous driving when the latent space is jointly supervised by language and a visual world model. It directly targets a key bottleneck in VLA systems: preserving reasoning quality while reaching answer-only latency.

top5.title: XEmbodied: A Foundation Model with Enhanced Geometric and Physical Cues for Large-Scale Embodied Environments
top5.summary: This paper tackles a central weakness of generic VLMs for embodied settings by integrating explicit 3D geometric and physical cues instead of treating them as side information. The combination of structured 3D adapters, physical-signal distillation, and broad 18-benchmark evaluation makes it one of the strongest general embodied-model releases in this batch.

top5.title: MultiWorld: Scalable Multi-Agent Multi-View Video World Models
top5.summary: MultiWorld extends video world modeling beyond the usual single-agent setup to joint multi-agent, multi-view prediction with controllability and cross-view consistency. That shift is important for robotics, games, and simulation, where realistic interaction and view coherence matter as much as frame quality.

top5.title: T-REN: Learning Text-Aligned Region Tokens Improves Dense Vision-Language Alignment and Scalability
top5.summary: T-REN addresses two important pain points at once: weak dense vision-language grounding and the token explosion that limits scaling to long videos. The reported gains on open-vocabulary segmentation, retrieval, and video parsing, while cutting token counts by 24x to 187x, make it a strong contribution for efficient multimodal perception.

top5.title: Asset Harvester: Extracting 3D Assets from Autonomous Driving Logs for Simulation
top5.summary: Asset Harvester is a system-level contribution aimed at converting sparse real-world driving observations into reusable, simulation-ready 3D assets. That makes it especially relevant for closed-loop AV simulation, where scene reconstruction alone is not enough for controllable object-level interaction and scalable testing.

top5.title: DINOv3 Beats Specialized Detectors: A Simple Foundation Model Baseline for Image Forensics
top5.summary: This paper makes a sharp practical claim: a simple DINOv3 plus LoRA baseline beats prior specialized forensic localization methods by large margins, including a 17-point average pixel-F1 gain under CAT-Net and much stronger performance in low-data settings. It is notable because it argues that foundation-model representations may already encode strong forensic cues, resetting the baseline for image forensics.

top5.title: Do Vision-Language Models Truly Perform Vision Reasoning? A Rigorous Study of the Modality Gap
top5.summary: CrossMath is a cleanly controlled benchmark that isolates text-only, image-only, and joint reasoning with identical task information, and the headline result is striking: visual input often hurts versus text alone. That makes this one of the more rigorous new pieces of evidence that many current VLMs still reason mostly in text space rather than from vision.

top5.title: Mind's Eye: A Benchmark of Visual Abstraction, Transformation and Composition for Multimodal LLMs
top5.summary: Mind's Eye targets visual abstraction and transformation with human-intelligence-test-style tasks, and humans reach 80 percent while top MLLMs stay below 50 percent. The benchmark is broad, cognitively motivated, and useful for pressure-testing whether multimodal systems can do more than pattern-match on standard VQA-style tasks.

top5.title: UniEditBench: A Unified and Cost-Effective Benchmark for Image and Video Editing via Distilled MLLMs
top5.summary: Image and video editing evaluation is still fragmented, and this benchmark unifies both modalities while also introducing distilled evaluator models that better align with human judgment at much lower cost. That combination of unified protocol and scalable judging could make it a practical reference point for editing research.

top5.title: Neural Gabor Splatting: Enhanced Gaussian Splatting with Neural Gabor for High-frequency Surface Reconstruction
top5.summary: This extends Gaussian splatting with per-primitive neural Gabor modeling and a frequency-aware densification strategy to better handle high-frequency textures and surfaces. It stands out because it tackles a real bottleneck in 3DGS representations while preserving the speed and editability that make splatting attractive.

top5.title: HY-World 2.0: A Multi-Modal World Model for Reconstructing, Generating, and Simulating 3D Worlds
top5.summary: HY-World 2.0 is the broadest systems paper in today's batch: it unifies text, image, multi-view, and video inputs into open 3D world reconstruction and generation with a full 3DGS pipeline and interactive renderer. The combination of open weights, end-to-end world building, and near-closed-model performance makes it especially notable for researchers working on world models and 3D scene generation.

top5.title: TokenGS: Decoupling 3D Gaussian Prediction from Pixels with Learnable Tokens
top5.summary: TokenGS rethinks feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting by replacing pixel-bound prediction with learnable Gaussian tokens and direct 3D coordinate regression. That architectural shift improves robustness to pose noise and multi-view inconsistency while reaching state-of-the-art reconstruction on static and dynamic scenes, making it a strong conceptual advance for 3DGS.

top5.title: GlobalSplat: Efficient Feed-Forward 3D Gaussian Splatting via Global Scene Tokens
top5.summary: GlobalSplat attacks the same 3DGS bottleneck from a different angle, using compact global scene tokens to avoid redundancy and representation bloat in feed-forward reconstruction. The reported 4MB footprint and sub-78ms inference make it particularly interesting for scalable, deployable novel-view synthesis.

top5.title: Geometrically Consistent Multi-View Scene Generation from Freehand Sketches
top5.summary: This paper opens a genuinely hard new task: generating consistent multi-view scenes from a single freehand sketch, where the input is abstract and geometrically distorted. The dataset construction, geometry-aware adapters, and explicit correspondence supervision make it one of the most novel vision-generation papers in the batch.

top5.title: Chain-of-Glimpse: Search-Guided Progressive Object-Grounded Reasoning for Video Understanding
top5.summary: Chain-of-Glimpse is notable because it pushes video reasoning toward explicit object-grounded evidence search rather than pure object-agnostic inference. Its stepwise grounded traces and cross-benchmark gains make it a strong signal paper for interpretable multimodal reasoning in video.

top5.title: SpatialEvo: Self-Evolving Spatial Intelligence via Deterministic Geometric Environments
top5.summary: Uses geometry-derived ground truth as a deterministic supervision signal for improving 3D spatial reasoning, avoiding the drift of self-confirming pseudo-labels and showing broad benchmark gains.

top5.title: Geometric Context Transformer for Streaming 3D Reconstruction
